chat.author: Chunchi Liu
chat.text: Registry entries are self-registered or consensus-based?

chat.author: Denis Avrilionis
chat.text: The access to the registry can be permissioned or permissionless depending on the use case

chat.author: Venkatraman Ramakrishna

chat.author: Denis Avrilionis

chat.author: Martin Gfeller
chat.text: https://vlei.com/

chat.author: David Millman
chat.text: How does one join the discussions on Stage Zero?

chat.author: Denis Avrilionis
chat.text: David, send an email to the satp mailing list about joining stage zero discussion

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: @David: I will ping you.

chat.author: Martin Gfeller
chat.text: @Denis, how detailed do the semantics need to be? In legacy finance, somebody once estimated there are 30 K  attributes defining semantics in fixed income worldwide.

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: The EU has the new mandate for Business eWallets, which will require the eWallet interact with services (Registries) with standardized APIs.

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: @Martin: We are following the attributes defined in ISO20022.

chat.author: Martin Gfeller
chat.text: AFAIK ISO20022 is for transactions, not defining the assets themselves.

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: Correct.  For assets, the DTCC has a long list of assets (RWA).

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: Asset classes (non-currency)

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: Today, there is no JSON-based representation of these asset claasses.

chat.author: Wes Hardaker
chat.text: We can fix that!!!

chat.author: Martin Gfeller
chat.text:
Legacy finance distinguishes between two types of assets: 

Over the counter, agreed between two parties, with some standards like ISDA
Assets registered with somebody, e.g., Stocks, Bonds, Futures, etc., which have a globally recognized id, which can be used to look up the definition. 

I believe we cannot go into the OTC group, because there are simply too many variations. For the 2nd one, you only need an id and a pointer.

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: @Martin: agree on ISDA (over the counter). The 2nd type much more flexible.

chat.author: Martin Gfeller
chat.text: Yes, and the 2nd is kind of opaque (you don't need to know the details of the contract).

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: Figure 4 in this paper may help understanding the TAR: https://www.techrxiv.org/doi/full/10.36227/techrxiv.174060358.86907117/v1

chat.author: Martin Gfeller
chat.text: I think you're 100% on the right track - we just need to document also what it is not (standardizing all assets).

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: glancing quickly at the openapi spec, version numbers are highly discouraged in HTTP apis in the IETF.

chat.author: Thomas Hardjono
chat.text: Thanks Wes, Claire & Peter.

chat.author: Denis Avrilionis
chat.text: Thank you all!

chat.author: Venkatraman Ramakrishna
chat.text: Thank you all!
